18:5.arabic: مَّا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنْ عِلْمٍ وَلَا لِـَٔابَآئِهِمْ ۚ كَبُرَتْ كَلِمَةً تَخْرُجُ مِنْ أَفْوَٰهِهِمْ ۚ إِن يَقُولُونَ إِلَّا كَذِبًا
18:5.english: They have no knowledge of it, nor did their fathers. How great a word that comes from their mouths — they speak nothing but a lie.
18:5.score_notes: Surah Al-Kahf — retranslated to 'great' not 'grave' to keep root connection visible.

lesson_use.heading: 9 · كَبُرَتْ (how great)
lesson_use.arabic: **كَبُرَتْ** كَلِمَةً تَخْرُجُ مِنْ أَفْوَاهِهِمْ ۚ إِن يَقُولُونَ إِلَّا كَذِبًا
lesson_use.english: How **great** a word that comes from their mouths — they speak nothing but a lie
lesson_use.notes: Notice **كَبُرَتْ** — the same root verb you met earlier, now with a feminine ending. The root letters kāf-bā-rā are right there.

18:110.arabic: قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ
18:110.english: Say: I am only a human like you — it is revealed to me that your God is one God.
18:110.score_notes: Closing of Surah Al-Kahf — recited every Friday. Too long for Learn but good for Practice. Audio already downloaded.

lesson_use.rejection_reason: Too long. Pattern dropped from v2 table.
